rationale: In the citing paper, Steenburgh (2007) is used in the hypothesis-development section rather than as a generic background reference. It provides specific conceptual support for the claim that share-based output measures treat market expansion differently, which directly motivates H4 and one of the meta-analysis moderators. The citing study does not reuse the full decomposition methodology, but the cited paper still meaningfully informs the conceptual setup of expected elasticity differences.
relation_description: The citing meta-analysis applies Steenburgh (2007)'s distinction between absolute-demand and share-based interpretations to predict that personal selling elasticities will be lower when output is measured as relative share. This is a transfer of a conceptual elasticity-decomposition insight into a new empirical synthesis context.
